description.current: Block premature completion: require personally examined evidence, specific citations, criteria matching, regression checks, final state capture. Use before marking tasks done, closing gates.
issues: Vague 'use before marking tasks done'—no explicit hook into TaskUpdate status changes
Doesn't explain WHO enforces this (Claude? Hook? Manual discipline?)
Missing guidance on what 'final state' means when task spans multiple commits

issues.description: The checklist (lines 32-57) uses vague 'Good vs Bad' comparisons without explaining the *why*. Why is 'examined the evidence file' better than 'report about it'? Discipline requires clarity on the reasoning.

Evidence citation format (lines 60-65) is introduced but relies heavily on a reference file (evidence-citation-examples.md) that should have anti-patterns inline. Circular reference: skill says 'see references/' but could be self-contained.
'When to Apply' section (lines 71-77) lists 5 scenarios but doesn't say how Claude is supposed to enforce this—is there a hook that blocks TaskUpdate? A guard in the verdicts? Mechanism is unclear.

Scope section (lines 16-19) says what this skill does NOT handle (evidence generation, planning) but those are exactly the things a user will ask about in practice. Lack of integration diagram creates cognitive load.
Related Skills section (lines 84-88) is accurate but minimal—no examples of how this skill chains with functional-validation or e2e-validate in a real workflow.

strengths: The core rule (lines 22-29) is clear and imperative—5 concrete steps that block hand-wavy claims
Evidence-citation-examples.md reference file is excellent—anti-patterns table is specific and actionable
Checklist format is self-policing—users can run through it independently
Security policy (lines 79-82) is explicit: this is read-only, prevents false completions

bundled_resources.missing_scripts_opportunity: A small auto-checker script (e.g., verify-evidence.sh) could validate that every criterion in a requirements table has a matching evidence citation. Currently manual.

top_fixes: Add a verification hook into the Claude Code task completion flow—automatically remind users to run the checklist before marking tasks done
Inline anti-patterns table from evidence-citation-examples.md into the main body (line 35–50) instead of referencing it—core logic should be self-contained
Clarify enforcement mechanism: Is this a reminder skill? Or a blocker skill? Does Claude refuse to mark tasks complete without evidence?
Add a workflow diagram showing how this skill chains with e2e-validate (generates evidence) → this skill (examines evidence) → verdict-writer (produces final report)

test_prompts.prompt: I implemented the user login form, ran the e2e test, got PASS. Can I mark this task done?
test_prompts.expected_output: Skill runs through checklist. User says 'I saw the e2e test pass' but hasn't personally examined the evidence file. Skill demands: Open e2e-evidence/login-form.png, describe exactly what you see (form fields, buttons, states). Until user provides specific observation ('Email field with blue border, password field, blue Submit button'), mark done rejected.

test_prompts.prompt: The API endpoint returns 200 and the response looks right to me. I also updated the frontend to use the new fields. Is that enough to ship?
test_prompts.expected_output: Skill asks: Did you check for regressions? Did you verify the final state is actually the state you're shipping? (E.g., if you made 3 commits, did you re-capture evidence after the last one?) User must cite: e2e-evidence/api-endpoint.json with full response body, e2e-evidence/ui-with-new-fields.png showing fields rendered, regression-check.txt showing no prior journey broke.

test_prompts.prompt: My subagent ran validation and reported PASS on all 6 journeys. The task is done, right?
test_prompts.expected_output: Skill rejects delegation: You must personally examine the subagent's evidence files. Open each one, describe what you see. For each of 6 journeys: What specific observation proves it passed? (E.g., 'journey-login screenshot shows dashboard rendered with user's name in header'). Delegation without re-examination is not allowed.
